title: 2026 F1 Japanese GP RACE analysis by Peter Windsor
description: Kimi Antonelli didn't make a great start from the Suzuka pole but he recovered well before taking full advantage of a timely Safety Car on lap 22: back in front at the restart, with Oscar Piastri's McLaren-Mercedes now in his mirrors, the young Italian then disappeared into the Japanese sunset, setting fastest lap as he secured his second win in a row. Oscar duly finished a gritty P2, beating Charles Leclerc's Ferrari to the line by 1.5sec, while George Russell could finish only P4 for Mercedes

2026 F1 Japanese GP FRIDAY Practice  Analysis
description:
2026 F1 Japanese GP FRIDAY Practice  Analysis. 

Oscar Piastri tops Friday practice at Suzuka, leading both Mercedes drivers in a session that’s already exposing the circuit’s usual high-speed challenges. McLaren look especially strong through the Esses and into Degner, with Piastri’s balance allowing him to carry consistent minimum speed through the long-direction changes.

description:

title: Verstappen And Red Bull’s Massive Problems Explained | F1 Explainer
description:
What has happened to Red Bull Racing and McLaren? The teams that once dominated Formula 1 now find themselves in deep trouble. In China, everything went wrong: Max Verstappen had issues and retired, Isack Hadjar spun, the RB22 lacks both speed and reliability, and McLaren did not even make it to the start. Meanwhile, Mercedes and Ferrari simply drove away from the field.

In this video, we analyse the major technical problems facing Red Bull and McLaren. From Max Verstappen’s disastrous weekend

title: 2026 F1 Chinese GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: New regs or not - McLaren-Mercedes in the race (or not): nothing could detract from the enormity of this day in Shanghai - from the first GP win for Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli. From the pole, he seized the race lead from Ferrari's Sir Lewis Hamilton on lap two - and was thereafter never headed.  Lewis re-applied pressure after the pit stops; and team-mate, George Russell, was up their in P2, pushing hard, as the race unfolded.  Kimi, though, was unperturbed.
